mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 8.0 percent, labor market tightening accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE drops below 8.0 within a 3-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth combined with rising residential housing starts signals synchronized real-economy expansion, which compresses equity risk premiums and lifts forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising inflation environment, equity markets price in higher nominal growth and asset revaluation, generating positive forward returns as long as the federal funds rate remains near zero.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns turn negative in more than 50% of observations where CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in housing starts reflects improving business and consumer confidence, which translates into higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for HOUST changes against 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year periods.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls exceed 130 million and remain stable, the labor market has reached a post-crisis equilibrium that supports steady equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not statistically higher than the unconditional mean when PAYEMS crosses above 130000 (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances alongside stable short-term interest rates indicate expanding household liquidity without financing cost pressure, which fuels equity demand and lifts forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising RSAFS and flat FEDFUNDS predicts negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of test windows.

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recoveries, a rapid rebound in industrial production outpaces lagging demand indicators, creating temporary supply gluts that suppress equity valuations over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 1.5% while GDPC1 growth < 2.0% annualized
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO accelerates faster than GDPC1.
